title: CLI
description: Install the you-agent-factory CLI and look up the core you commands for starting a factory, submitting work, checking sessions, and reading packaged docs.
openingSummary: The you-agent-factory CLI is the command-line entrypoint for installing the factory tooling and operating long-running agent work from your machine.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This reference covers the you-agent-factory CLI: how to install the you binary, and which core commands start a factory, submit work, inspect sessions and work, or print packaged docs. It is the durable CLI lookup surface for customers who need install entrypoints and command purpose at a glance.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A factory is the long-running runtime that keeps agent work persistent. Some commands start that runtime on your machine. Other commands talk to a factory that is already running. Packaged docs commands print reference markdown without needing a live factory.

install.title: Install
install.body: Install the you CLI with the published release entrypoint for your operating system. These commands install the binary; they do not start a factory.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Install the CLI, then use the command matrix below to choose the right you command. Start a factory when you need a local runtime. Submit work only after a factory is already running. Use session and work commands to inspect live state, and you docs when you need packaged reference topics without a live factory.

commands.title: Commands
commands.body: Use this matrix to pick a core you command and see whether a factory must already be running. Startup forms create or attach to a local runtime. Submit forms enqueue work against a live factory. Docs print packaged reference markdown offline.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web CLI reference for install entrypoints and core command purpose. It is not a full flag dump, not a sync of packaged CLI markdown, and not the harness, MCP, or configuration deep pages.

links.commandMatrixYouPurpose: Start the default local factory and dashboard from the current project.
links.commandMatrixYouRunDirPurpose: Start or attach to a local factory from an explicit factory directory.
links.commandMatrixYouRunNamedPurpose: Run a named or portable factory without guessing the local directory layout.
links.commandMatrixYouRunWorkPurpose: Submit batch JSON as part of local factory startup, not steady-state ingress.
links.commandMatrixYouRunWorkRunning: No when used with a startup form such as --dir.
links.commandMatrixYouSubmitPurpose: Submit one work item to a factory that is already accepting work.
links.commandMatrixYouSubmitBatchPurpose: Upsert batch JSON to a running factory session.
links.commandMatrixYouSessionListPurpose: Check whether a factory host has open sessions before submitting work.
links.commandMatrixYouSessionListRunning: Yes — talks to a running host.
links.commandMatrixYouWorkCommand: you work list / you work show
links.commandMatrixYouWorkPurpose: Inspect work items from a running factory.
links.commandMatrixYouDocsPurpose: Print packaged reference markdown from the installed binary.
